body.title: Drift detection: baseline check type for auditor + nanny continuous scheduling
body.result: baseline check kind in auditor + 3 seed checks (2 shipped, 1 deferred pending T-shim-traffic-spy) + nanny hourly schedule + FAIL→ticket; 30 unit tests pass
body.description:
Drift detection is a new check type for the auditor device (T-auditor-device): compare current agent behavior to a rolling baseline and flag significant deviations. The time-series data already exists (clan.budget_ledger for inference call rates, datacenter_logs/shim/trace/ for tool call rates).

New check kind: 'baseline' — defines a metric (sql query returning a count), a window (e.g. last 1 hour), a baseline window (e.g. 7-day rolling average same hour), and a threshold multiplier (e.g. 3x = flag if current > 3x baseline). Example: Igor's inference calls per hour > 3x his 7-day hourly average → drift finding → ticket filed.

Nanny integration: schedule auditor run_all(kind='baseline') every hour. Findings above threshold become tickets via the learning loop (T-learning-loop-generalized). No human needed unless a ticket is filed.

Seed checks on first deploy: inference call rate per agent, memory write rate per agent, tool call error rate per shim.

**Affected files:** devices/auditor/ (baseline check type), devices/nanny/ (hourly schedule), config/audit_checks/ (seed baseline checks)
**Design rules:** auditor device dependency (T-auditor-device must ship first); nanny owns scheduling
**Scope boundary:** IN — baseline check type, 3 seed checks, nanny hourly schedule, findings → tickets via learning loop; OUT — anomaly ML models, cross-agent correlation, alerting to Akien (nanny handles that separately)
**Completion criteria:** a seeded baseline check fires when inference rate exceeds 3x baseline; finding appears in auditor history; ticket filed automatically; nanny runs the check hourly without manual trigger
